news.headline: Peter Schiff criticizes Fed's 2% inflation target, calling it a pretext for monetary policy. Target was zero for first 99 years. Fed started targeting 2% inflation in 2012 with a concept from New Zealand in 1995. Fed Chair Powell raises persistent inflation concerns.
news.summary: Economist Peter Schiff criticized the Federal Reserve's 2% inflation target, claiming it is used to justify accommodative monetary policy. This comes after Fed Chair Jerome Powell voiced concerns about persistent inflation during a recent congressional testimony. Schiff argued that the 2% target, implemented in 2012, has enabled quantitative easing and zero interest rate policies, shifting from the previous unofficial target of zero for price stability in the first 99 years of the Fed's existence. Additionally, former central banker Neil Grossman provided historical context, mentioning that the 2% inflation idea originated with the Reserve Bank of New Zealand in 1995 and was later adopted by other central banks. Grossman implied that the Fed has utilized this target to overlook certain aspects of their monetary policy approach. To read the full story, visit Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Wondershare Filmora launches "Can You Feel the Beat?" music challenge on mobile app, featured on Google Play's homepage.
news.summary: In Vancouver, British Columbia on February 23, 2025, the popular video creativity software Wondershare Filmora announced the launch of the "Can You Feel the Beat?" music challenge on its mobile application. This challenge invites users to showcase their creativity using Filmora's extensive music library and cutting-edge AI editing features. The platform aims to empower users worldwide to produce distinctive video content.

news.headline: Bgin Blockchain, a crypto mining tech company, files for $50M IPO in US to raise funds through public offering.
news.summary: Bgin Blockchain, a manufacturer of mining rigs, has submitted paperwork for a public listing in the United States. The offering is projected to generate approximately $50 million in funds.

news.headline: Investors with losses in ModivCare Inc. urged to contact Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP for a securities lawsuit. ( Characters: 105 )

("ModivCare") (NASDAQ: MODV) by the law firm Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP. The lawsuit is on behalf of investors who bought or acquired shares. For more information, visit www.ktmc.com.

North Korean-linked funds, including $146 million, may flow to Bitcoin mixers next, warns Elliptic amid unprecedented $1.46B theft.
news.summary: A substantial portion of the unprecedented $1.46 billion theft by North Korean actors is being laundered through anonymous exchange services, according to Elliptic. Reports suggest that at least 10% of the stolen funds are being cleaned through these avenues.

news.headline: Cosco Shipping Drops 8.3% in Hong Kong on Trump Proposed Ship Fees
Chillers Market projected to reach $15.42 billion by 2029 at a 5.0% CAGR, driven by energy efficiency demands and green building regulations. Eco-friendly solutions gaining traction.
news.summary: The Chillers Market is projected to reach USD 15.42 billion by 2029, growing at a CAGR of 5.0% from USD 12.08 billion in 2024, driven by a rising demand for energy-efficient cooling solutions. Factors contributing to this growth include increased installation of HVAC systems due to expansion of construction activities, higher cooling needs in data centers, hospitals, and manufacturing units, and the adoption of low-GWP refrigerants and smart connectivity features. Government regulations promoting green buildings and energy efficiency, as well as consumer awareness on climate change, are also fueling the shift towards eco-friendly cooling solutions. Key players in the market include Daikin Industries, Trane Technologies, Carrier, Johnson Controls Inc., and others. The market report includes 274 Market Data Tables, 55 Figures, and spans 292 Pages. A major driver is the growing demand for process cooling in food and healthcare applications, while high initial capital investment poses a restraint. Opportunities for manufacturers lie in producing eco-friendly chillers, but they also face challenges in performance in complex applications. (Characters: 962)

news.headline: Fangzhou Inc. (06086.HK) included in Hang Seng Composite Index, a leader in Internet healthcare solutions.
news.summary: In Hong Kong on February 23, 2025, Fangzhou Inc. (06086.HK), a prominent provider of Internet healthcare solutions, revealed that it has been chosen to be part of the Hang Seng Composite Index by Hang Seng Indexes. This inclusion signifies the company's growing significance in the market and reflects its strong performance.

Huangshan city in Anhui province becomes top China destination for global tourists due to scenic beauty and cultural appeal.
news.summary: On February 21, 2025, it was reported that Huangshan city in East China's Anhui province has become the top choice for international tourists looking to discover China. Known for its stunning landscapes, abundant cultural treasures, and excellent hospitality services, Huangshan city has captured the interest of visitors from abroad.

Bill Lowen wins 2025 FXR Pro Fish Bassmaster Elite at St. Johns with 73lbs, 14oz, 2nd trophy.
news.summary: In Palatka, Florida on February 23, 2025, Bill Lowen faced obstacles from vegetation twice within three days during the 2025 FXR Pro Fish Bassmaster Elite at St. Johns. Despite the challenges, the pro angler from Brookville, Indiana, showcased persistence to emerge victorious with a total weight of 73 pounds and 14 ounces after the four-day competition.

news.headline: LG Display starts mass production of industry's first 40-inch P2P automotive display, revolutionizing driving experience.
news.summary: In Seoul, South Korea, on February 23, 2025, LG Display revealed the commencement of mass production for the automotive sector's pioneering 40-inch Pillar to Pillar (P2P) display. This innovation marks a significant advancement in display technologies.LG Display is a global leader in this field and is intensifying efforts to enhance its position in the market.

news.headline: Global study finds 80% manufacturing firms faced more cyber attacks with potential downtime cost of US$2M; only 50% well-prepared in security.
news.summary: A recent global study conducted by Omdia in Singapore revealed that individual firms are facing estimated downtime costs of up to US$2 million. The study showed that 80% of manufacturing companies have witnessed a notable rise in security incidents or breaches. It was also highlighted that relying solely on traditional methods like "air gapping" is no longer enough to safeguard against these threats.

news.headline: Arrowpoint Investment Partners picks SS&C for fund admin. SS&C GlobeOp to administer $1 billion multi-strategy fund.
news.summary: In Windsor, Conn. on Feb. 23, 2025, SS&C Technologies Holdings, Inc. revealed that Arrowpoint Investment Partners, a multi-strategy hedge fund investment firm in Singapore, has opted for SS&C GlobeOp for administering its primary $1 billion multi-strategy fund.

Montana House lawmakers reject bill for a Bitcoin reserve, voting 41-59 against the proposal.
news.summary: The proposal to create a Bitcoin reserve in Montana was rejected by the state's House of Representatives in a vote of 41 to 59. This decision means that the US state will not move forward with the plan to establish a reserve of the digital currency.

news.summary: Discover how to efficiently generate comprehensive meeting summaries utilizing AssemblyAI's LeMUR framework and large language models through a concise Python script of only five lines. This streamlined process allows for the creation of detailed summaries with minimal coding complexity. Explore further by accessing additional information on this innovative approach.

news.headline: Use Python and AssemblyAI API to summarize meetings in minutes, cutting review time significantly. #AI #meetingsummaries
news.summary: Learn about streamlining meeting summaries with the use of Python and AssemblyAI's API, which can significantly decrease the time taken to review recordings by leveraging AI-driven techniques. This innovative approach can lead to saving substantial amounts of time and enhancing productivity.

news.headline: Franklin Templeton seeks SEC approval for Solana-based funds as one of many Solana ETF applicants.
news.summary: Franklin Templeton, an asset manager, has requested approval from the SEC for investment funds tied to the performance of Solana. Solana stands as the sixth-largest cryptocurrency by market capitalization.

news.headline: New America High Income Fund to reorganize into T. Rowe Price High Yield Fund; expected to close on mutually agreed date.
news.summary: On February 21, 2025, The New America High Income Fund, Inc. (NYSE: HYB) announced the reorganization of the fund into the T. Rowe Price High Yield Fund. The initial expectation was for the reorganization to close after the New York Stock Exchange's closing on the same day, but the new closing date will be determined in writing by both parties. This is subject to meeting standard closing conditions. After the reorganization, shareholders of the Fund will hold Investor Class shares of the T. Rowe Price Fund.

Pizza icon Patsy Grimaldi, 93, passes away. Known for coal-oven pizza near Brooklyn Bridge, his place attracted NYC & global diners.
news.summary: The coal-oven pizzeria located near the Brooklyn Bridge has attracted customers from various places, including New York City.

news.headline: Dr. Gregory George MD PhD, Mesoblast's major shareholder, joins the board; appointment announced on Feb 23, 2025.
news.summary: In New York on February 23, 2025, Mesoblast Limited, a leading company in allogeneic cellular medicines for inflammatory diseases traded on Nasdaq under MESO and ASX under MSB, named Dr. Gregory George MD PhD, who is the company's primary shareholder, to its Board of Directors.

news.headline: Global composite dental restorative material market to hit $887.02M by 2032 at 7.2% CAGR, driven by prevalence of dental diseases and aesthetic procedures. High cost of procedures limits growth.
news.summary: The global composite dental restorative material market was valued at $474.00 million in 2023 and is projected to reach $887.02 million by 2032, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 7.2% from 2024 to 2032. The market is segmented by type (high viscosity composite, low viscosity composite, universal composite), form (capsules, syringe, others), end user (hospitals, dental clinics, others), and distribution channel (online, offline). Key drivers for market growth include the rise in dental diseases and increased adoption of aesthetic procedures, while the high cost of procedures poses a restraint to growth. Advancements in technology are expected to bring new opportunities. The report from Allied Market Research covers these aspects extensively across 350 pages, with forecasts from 2024 to 2032.

news.headline: Transition to multipolar world discussed at Munich Security Conference. Focus on potential disorder and governance issues.
news.summary: During the 61st Munich Security Conference (MSC), which took place recently, discussions centered around the concept of multipolarity. The main question addressed was whether the current global landscape is shifting towards a multipolar system. Concerns were raised regarding the potential consequences of multipolarity, with worries about increased disorder and challenges in governance. The event gathered key stakeholders from various countries and organizations to delve into these critical issues and explore possible implications.

news.headline: Pfizer discontinues hemophilia gene therapy Beqvez due to limited interest from patients & doctors. FDA approved in April 2024.
news.summary: Pfizer Inc. (NYSE:PFE) has reportedly halted the global development and commercialization of its hemophilia B gene therapy, Beqvez, as per various news outlets on Thursday. Beqvez was approved by the FDA in April 2024 for moderate to severe hemophilia B in adult patients who are using factor IX prophylaxis therapy or have a history of life-threatening hemorrhage or serious spontaneous bleeding episodes, and lack neutralizing antibodies to adeno-associated virus serotype Rh74var capsid. Pfizer stated to Nikkei Asia that the decision was influenced by the limited interest shown by patients and healthcare providers in hemophilia gene therapies. For more information, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Wall Street anticipates Fed's hawkish meeting, unsure if done cutting rates. Comparisons to 1996 S&P 500.
news.summary: Investors on Wall Street are preparing for a Federal Reserve meeting that is expected to be more hawkish than previously anticipated. The meeting may resemble the market conditions of 1996 for the S&P 500. There is a sense that the upcoming rate cut could be the final one.

news.headline: Fed cuts rates with 2 more expected in 2025, causing S&P 500 to drop on doubts about dovish signals.
news.summary: The Federal Reserve reduced interest rates as anticipated and projected two additional cuts for 2025. However, the S&P 500 took a downturn amid skepticism regarding the Fed's commitment to implementing its accommodating policy indications.

news.headline: Fed meeting led to soaring Treasury yields and S&P 500 dip; linked to Trump's agenda. Stocks may get 'sloppy.'
news.summary: The recent Federal Reserve meeting caused a significant increase in Treasury yields and a decline in the S&P 500, largely due to the implications of President-elect Trump's agenda. This event has put Trump on notice regarding potential economic shifts. The market response may lead to a period of instability in stock trading.

news.headline: Fed's core PCE price index showed minimal increase in Nov, calming after high readings. S&P 500 surged.
news.summary: In November, the core PCE price index, the Federal Reserve's main gauge of inflation, experienced a modest increase following a period of higher readings. As a result, the S&P 500 surged.

news.headline: Bond market vigilantes sending warning to Trump, Powell as Treasury yields rise due to "reckless" policies.
news.summary: The rise in Treasury yields is being attributed to concerns among bond market participants, commonly known as bond market vigilantes, who are apprehensive about what they perceive as irresponsible fiscal and monetary measures under the leadership of former President Donald Trump and Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell. Market strategist Ed Yardeni highlighted the surge in Treasury yields as a clear indication of the vigilantes' unease, with the move being seen as a prominent signal to Trump and Powell. This development is perceived as a warning sign by investors, as reflected in an article from Investor's Business Daily.

news.headline: Top Wall Street analysts provide new price expectations for Westlake (NYSE:WLK) ahead of Q4 earnings. Earnings forecasted at $1.17/share (up from 53 cents last year) on $2.97 billion revenue. Shares fell 1.9% to $111.40 after weaker Q3 earnings. Visit Benzinga for more info.
news.summary: Westlake Corporation (NYSE:WLK) is set to reveal its fourth-quarter financial results on Monday, Feb. 24, 2025, before the market opens. Analysts are forecasting quarterly earnings of $1.17 per share for the Houston, Texas-based company, a significant increase from the 53 cents per share reported in the same period last year. Westlake anticipates quarterly revenue to reach $2.97 billion, up from $2.83 billion in the previous year. Following weaker-than-expected third-quarter earnings announced on Nov. 5, Westlake's stock price dropped by 1.9% to close at $111.40 on Thursday. For further insights into analyst ratings, readers can visit the Analyst Stock Ratings page on Benzinga where they can filter results by stock ticker, company name, analyst firm, rating change, and other factors.

news.headline: Trump denies tariff shift report, but S&P 500, Nucor, FCX, Enphase, and Ford stocks rise.
news.summary: The report suggesting President-elect Trump's tariffs could target strategic industries was denied by Trump. Despite this, there was a notable increase in the stock prices of key companies - the S&P 500, Nucor, FCX, Enphase, and Ford experienced growth. The market reacted positively as Nucor and FCX led a rally in the S&P 500 following Trump's denial of the tariffs report. This development was highlighted in an article on Investor's Business Daily.

news.headline: 10-year Treasury at 8-month high on rising job openings and ISM services data. S&P 500 drops as yield climbs.
news.summary: Unexpectedly, there was a significant increase in job openings, coinciding with accelerated activity in the ISM services gauge. This caused a drop in the S&P 500 as the 10-year Treasury yield continued to rise, reaching an 8-month high.

news.headline: Trump and Putin's productive call leads to oil sell-off. Oil prices drop by 2.7%, lowest since 2024. Peace negotiations for Ukraine announced.
news.summary: A call between President Trump and President Putin led to market turbulence with oil prices falling, including West Texas Intermediate crude dropping by 2.7% by NY afternoon. Trump announced potential peace talks for Ukraine. They discussed various topics and Trump assigned top officials including Secretary of State Rubio for negotiations. Putin responded positively to cooperation. These developments affected U.S. oil and defense stocks as markets reacted to the surprise announcement.

news.headline: Jobs report shows increased hiring, lower unemployment; S&P 500 falls. Fed likely to hold.
news.summary: The latest jobs report revealed an unexpected increase in payroll gains alongside a decrease in the unemployment rate. Simultaneously, S&P 500 futures experienced a decline. This solid hiring spree and lower unemployment rate are anticipated to influence the Federal Reserve's decisions, possibly prompting them to maintain their current position.

news.headline: Insulet's Q4 sales hit $597.5M, EPS $1.15 (exceeding $1.02 consensus). Omnipod revenue soared, reaching $585.7M.
news.summary: Insulet Corporation (NASDAQ: PODD) announced its fourth-quarter financial results on Thursday, revealing an adjusted EPS of $1.15, lower than the previous year's $1.40 but surpassing the estimated $1.02. The company's sales for the quarter amounted to $597.5 million, marking a 17.2% year-over-year increase (17.1% in constant currency), which exceeded the consensus of $582.81 million and the projected 12% to 15% growth range in constant currency.Specifically, the total revenue from Omnipod, Insulet's automated insulin delivery technology, reached $585.7 million, up by 16.9% (or 16.8% in constant currency). Revenue from U.S. Omnipod sales experienced a 12.4% rise, hitting $443.7 million, while International Omnipod revenue surged by 33.5% (or 33.1% in constant currency) to $142.0 million.Furthermore, the company reported Drug Delivery revenue of $11.8 million, indicating a substantial increase of 34.1%. For more details on this report and information on the gross margin, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Core CPI inflation decreases in December. S&P 500 rises as hopes for Fed rate cut increase.
news.summary: In December, core inflation, compared to a year prior, surprisingly decreased marginally. This development led to speculation that the Federal Reserve may consider cutting interest rates, prompting a notable increase in the S&P 500 index. This information was reported by Investor's Business Daily.

news.headline: Big banks may need to deal with cryptocurrencies despite reluctance. Republicans and Trump prioritize "debanking" issue.
news.summary: Republicans have heightened focus on "debanking," with the Trump administration making it an official priority. This entails scrutinizing and possibly cutting ties with financial institutions that are perceived as playing a role against conservative values. The move signifies a shift as Republicans ramp up efforts, with a notable push from the White House. The exact extent and impact of this initiative remain to be seen, as it becomes a prominent aspect under the current administration's leadership.
